page: Hetzner Cloud ccx53 is a dedicated CPU virtual server instance powered by the AMD EPYC Milan processor architecture (x86_64). It features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 16 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The processor includes 32 MB of L3 cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 32 KB of L1d cache. Memory capacity is configured at 125.0 GB of RAM, yielding approximately 3.91 GB per core. Local storage is provided via a 600 GB SSD, and the instance runs on the QEMU hypervisor. No GPUs are included in this configuration.

In benchmark evaluations, the ccx53 demonstrates strong single-core performance, highlighted by a stress-ng single-core score of 3066.19 and a PassMark CPU score of 47556.41, both placing in the top 25%. Cryptographic capabilities are also strong, with a PassMark CPU encryption test score of 39134.55. Memory performance is robust, with a PassMark memory score of 2801.37 in the top 25%, though large-block uncached memory read and write bandwidths remain in the average tier at 84.57 GB/sec and 62.84 GB/sec respectively. Small-model LLM inference is highly efficient, achieving top-tier text generation speeds of 360.07 tokens/sec for a 135M parameter model. However, performance drops significantly for larger models, with 70B parameter model text generation falling into the bottom 10% at 2.36 tokens/sec.

From a resource density perspective, the dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent performance without noisy-neighbor interference, representing a stable hardware profile for production environments. The balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes this instance well-suited for general-purpose workloads, multi-threaded compilation tasks, medium-scale database operations, and static web serving. It is also appropriate for running smaller machine learning models or text processing pipelines, though workloads requiring heavy GPU acceleration or massive memory footprints should look to specialized instances.
description: Hetzner Cloud ccx53 is a dedicated virtual server built on the x86_64 AMD EPYC Milan architecture, featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs across 16 physical cores and 125.0 GB of RAM. This configuration includes a 600 GB local SSD and operates under the QEMU hypervisor without GPU acceleration. Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core capabilities, with a PassMark CPU score of 47556.41 and a single-core stress-ng score of 3066.19, both ranking in the top 25%. While memory bandwidth and multi-core performance sit in the average tier, the instance excels at small-model LLM text generation, reaching 360.07 tokens/sec. Conversely, large-model inference is a notable bottleneck, yielding only 2.36 tokens/sec for 70B models. Qualitatively, the dedicated CPU allocation provides reliable, predictable compute resources. This server is suited for general-purpose application hosting, software compilation, database operations, and lightweight machine learning inference.
og_description: Hetzner Cloud ccx53 is a dedicated virtual server featuring 32 AMD EPYC Milan vCPUs, 125 GB RAM, and a 600 GB SSD. It delivers strong single-core CPU and encryption performance for production workloads.
meta_description: Hetzner Cloud ccx53 is a dedicated AMD EPYC Milan server with 32 vCPUs, 125 GB RAM, and 600 GB SSD storage, optimized for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC Milan server offering thirty-two vCPUs, balanced memory, and local SSD storage for predictable production workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan vCPUs with hyper-threading
125.0 GB RAM and 600 GB of local SSD storage
Strong single-core CPU and cryptographic benchmark performance
Top-tier text generation speeds for small-scale LLM inference
Dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent, non-shared resource performance
Suitable for general-purpose hosting, databases, and compilation tasks
